title: Lepidogrammitis rostrata
taxonomy: Lepidogrammitis rostrata is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which includes various species of ferns. This classification places Lepidogrammitis rostrata within the division Pteridophyta, or vascular non-seed plants. Lepidogrammitis rostrata is also classified as part of the order Polypodiales and the subclass Polypodiidae. Further classification places it within the class Polypodiopsida and the phylum Pteridophyta. The classification of Lepidogrammitis rostrata reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species.
uses: Lepidogrammitis rostrata is a type of fern native to Australia, and research on its potential medicinal uses is ongoing. However, there is limited information available on its specific applications. Lepidogrammitis rostrata has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, but these uses are not well-documented. Further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and potential benefits for human health. As a result, Lepidogrammitis rostrata is not commonly used in modern medicine.
